response:
Here are comprehensive unit tests for the `is_balanced_parens` function using pytest style, covering happy path, edge cases, and error cases:

```python
import pytest
from your_module import is_balanced_parens  # Replace 'your_module' with the actual module name

def test_happy_path_balanced():
    """Test with balanced parentheses of all types."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("()") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("[]") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("{}") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("()[]{}") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("([{}])") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("{[]}()") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("([{}([])]{})") == True

def test_happy_path_unbalanced():
    """Test with unbalanced parentheses of all types."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("(]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("([)]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("({[}])") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens(")(") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("}") == False

def test_edge_case_empty_string():
    """Test with an empty string."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("") == True

def test_edge_case_no_parentheses():
    """Test with a string containing no parentheses."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("abc123") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("a(b)c") == False  # Note: This is actually unbalanced if we consider the 'b' as content

def test_edge_case_nested_parentheses():
    """Test with deeply nested parentheses."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("((((()))))") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("[[[[[]]]]]") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("{{{{{{}}}}}") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("((((())))") == False  # Unbalanced

def test_edge_case_mixed_nested():
    """Test with mixed and nested parentheses."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("({[]})") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("([{}])") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("{[()]}") == True
    assert is_balanced_parens("({[}])") == False  # Unbalanced

def test_error_case_single_closing():
    """Test with a single closing parenthesis."""
    assert is_balanced_parens(")") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("}") == False

def test_error_case_single_opening():
    """Test with a single opening parenthesis."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("(") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("[") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("{") == False

def test_error_case_mismatched_pairs():
    """Test with mismatched pairs of parentheses."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("(]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("([)]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("{[}]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("}") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("{") == False

def test_error_case_interleaved_unbalanced():
    """Test with interleaved unbalanced parentheses."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("([)]") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("({)}") == False
    assert is_balanced_parens("{[}]") == False

def test_error_case_non_parenthesis_characters():
    """Test with strings containing non-parenthesis characters."""
    assert is_balanced_parens("a(b)c") == False  # 'b' is not a parenthesis, but '(' and ')' are balanced
    assert is_balanced_parens("abc") == True  # No parentheses
    assert is_balanced_parens("a(b[c]d)e") == False  # Balanced parentheses but non-parenthesis characters
